conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture (chordal modulator characterization for even-hole-free graphs)
paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions VIII. Excluding a forest in (theta, prism)-free graphs
summary: The conjecture that every chordal graph of clique number at most three modulates even-hole-free graphs (the converse of Theorem 1.2 of Sintiari–Trotignon) remains open in full generality. The source paper itself proved the case when H is a forest (Theorem 1.6). Subsequently, Hajebi (2024/2025) proved the conjecture for H = cone(cone(F)) for any forest F and for H a 'crystal' (a graph built from coned double stars), substantially extending the known special cases, but the full statement for all K₄-free chordal H is still open.

since_posted.title: Chordal graphs, even-hole-free graphs and sparse obstructions to bounded treewidth
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture (in the equivalent treewidth-obstruction formulation) for H = cone(cone(F)) for any forest F and for H a crystal, extending the t=4 and H=cone(F) special cases; the full conjecture for all K₄-free chordal H remains open.

search_queries: chordal graph clique number modulates even-hole-free Abrishami Chudnovsky Spirkl
arXiv 2301.02138 even-hole-free chordal modulator conjecture follow-up
Sintiari Trotignon even-hole-free modulator chordal clique number conjecture 2023 2024 2025
notes: The conjecture from 2301.02138 is equivalent to the main Conjecture 1.8 of arXiv:2401.01299 (bounded-treewidth characterization for even-hole-free graphs): 'K₄-free chordal graphs' equals 'chordal graphs of clique number at most three'. arXiv:2401.01299 proves new cases (Theorems 1.11 and 1.12) but the full conjecture remains open. A strengthening (Conjecture 1.14 in 2401.01299) was disproved by Chudnovsky and Trotignon via a new variant of layered wheels, but this does not affect the main conjecture.
